title: System for intracorporeal transillumination of tissue by remote control of light emitting diodes
abstract: A remotely controlled device which illuminates internal tissue and organs of a patient is described. The device includes a light-emitting diode or a plurality of light-emitting diodes ("LEDs"), which may be individually or collectively controlled to emit light at a selected intensity, a selected color, or with a selected display pattern. The remote controllable LED or plurality of LEDs provides improved control for a surgeon or other medical provider to assist with procedures within the body. These procedures include those involving the reproductive organs of males and females, gastric and bariatrics, and other structures in the abdomen or other body region.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS It is claimed: 1. A device for in vivo illumination of a tissue, comprising: an elongate member having a proximal end and a distal end, the distal end comprising a plurality of light emitting diodes (LEDs), wherein one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs are configurable to emit light at an intensity within a range of intensities; a user interface for receiving a user input associated with the intensity of the light; and a controller communicatively coupled to the plurality of LEDs and the user interface, wherein the controller is configured to adjust the intensity of light emitted by the one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs based on the user input. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs emits light at an intensity that is configurable based on the type of surgery. 3. The device of claim 1, wherein the one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs emits light at an intensity that is configurable to give an indication of a thickness of the tissue. 4. The device of claim 1, wherein the intensity of light emitted by the one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs comprises a portion of LEDs in the plurality of LEDs configured to controllable emit light at an intensity different from another portion of LEDs in the plurality of LEDs. 5. The device of claim 1, wherein the intensity of light emitted by the plurality of LEDs is determined by configuring a first portion of LEDs in the plurality of LEDs to emit light at a selected intensity and a second portion of LEDs in the plurality of LEDs to emit light at a different selected intensity. 6. The device of claim 1, wherein the controller is integral with the device. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the controller is separate from the device. 8. The device of claim 1, further comprising one or more of a positioning member, a lumen, and an inflatable balloon. 9. A system for in vivo illumination of a tissue, comprising: 

 an elongate member having a proximal end and a distal end comprising a plurality of light emitting diodes (LEDs), wherein one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs are configurable to emit light at an intensity within a range of intensities, to emit light in a pattern and/or to emit light of a selected color; and a controller communicatively coupled to the plurality of LEDs, the controller comprising: a user interface for receiving a user input associated with the intensity of the light; and a set of instructions executable to adjust the intensity of light emitted by the plurality of LEDs based on the user input. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein the one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs emits light at an intensity that is configurable based on the type of surgery. 11. The system of claim 9, wherein the one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs emits light at an intensity that is configurable to give an indication of a thickness of the tissue. 12. The system of claim 9, wherein the intensity of light emitted by the one or more of the LEDs in the plurality of LEDs comprises a portion of LEDs in the plurality of LEDs configured to controllable emit light at an intensity different from another portion of LEDs in the plurality of LEDs. 13. The system of claim 9, wherein the intensity of light emitted by the plurality of LEDs is determined by configuring a first portion of LEDs in the plurality of LEDs to emit light at a selected intensity and a second portion of LEDs in the plurality of LEDs to emit light at a different selected intensity. 14. The system of claim 9, wherein the controller is integral with the device. 15. The system of claim 9, wherein the controller is separate from the device. 16. The system of claim 9, further comprising one or more of a positioning member, a lumen, and an inflatable balloon.
